Thank you for adopting this Unengaged, U: Thank you for adopting this Unengaged, Unreached People Group. Your church is joining a global movement asking God to open the way for the gospel among people who currently have little or no access to it. This page provides resources to help your church stay informed, mobilize prayer, and explore how God may use your community to help bring the good news of Jesus to this people group.
Thank you for taking a step toward adopt: Thank you for taking a step toward adopting an unengaged people group. Please complete the form below so we can confirm your church's adoption, connect with your Champion, and begin sending prayer updates and resources.
Thank you for your adoption commitment!: Thank you for your adoption commitment! We will be in touch soon.
Thank you for your message. We will get: Thank you for your message. We will get back to you soon!
The Champion is the person who will orga: The Champion is the person who will organize the 144 intercessors and receive updates.

Unengaged people groups have no missiona: Unengaged people groups have no missionaries, no churches, and often no known believers. Adoption helps ensure they are finally seen, prayed for, and intentionally pursued with the gospel.
We chose this name because Jesus is wort: We chose this name because Jesus is worthy of glory from every tribe, tongue, people, and nation. DOXA reminds us that we partner with the whole Church to take the whole gospel to the whole world – until people from every nation are worshipping Jesus and He alone receives all the glory.
We recommend that churches give a monthl: We recommend that churches give a monthly pledge based on their congregational size.  i.e. $100 a month for a congregation around 100.  $500 a month for a congregation around 500. $1000 a month for a congregation around 1000, etc.
What is your commitment? Pray. Give. Sen: What is your commitment? Pray. Give. Send.
When we pray, we join believers around t: When we pray, we join believers around the world in God's mission, standing together for peoples still waiting to hear the name of Jesus.
When you adopt a people group, you step: When you adopt a people group, you step into a leadership role on their behalf—standing in the gap until the gospel takes root. This involves a commitment to:
